title: athlete — Eloquent observers
description: One observer enforces the module's write-time invariants: entitlement consumption, cross-tenant defense-in-depth, DOB immutability, medical + emergency-contact permission gating, age-group snapshot resolution, and status-machine transitions. The medical write path additionally consults the MedicalDisclosureGate binding — the observer only knows *whether* the caller carries the permission; the gate encapsulates *how* the check runs (permission + entitlement + optional guardian-consent verification for minors when consent_medical_disclosure is false).

handles: creating → populate ULID prefix `ath_`
creating → refuse when tenant does NOT hold `athlete.core` feature (defense-in-depth; all tiers have this, but the check exists in case a kill-switch flips)
creating → refuse when parent Branch.tenant_id != tenant_id (cross-tenant defense-in-depth)
creating → refuse when parent Branch.status != 'active' (returns `ATHLETE_BRANCH_NOT_ACTIVE`)
creating → refuse when user_id is set AND user.tenant_id != tenant_id (cross-tenant defense-in-depth on the user link)
creating → refuse when user_id is set AND another live Athlete row exists for (tenant_id, user_id) via the composite unique index; return `ATHLETE_USER_ALREADY_HAS_ACTIVE_ROW`
creating → refuse when external_id is set AND already exists for the tenant (partial unique index); return `ATHLETE_EXTERNAL_ID_TAKEN`
creating → validate date_of_birth via `valid_dob` rule (>= today - 100 years, <= today; refuses nonsense DOBs); refuse with `ATHLETE_DOB_OUT_OF_BOUNDS` otherwise
creating → validate athlete age (>= 3 AND <= 100 as of today) via `athlete_age_within_platform_bounds` rule
creating → consume 1 unit of `athlete_slot` entitlement against (application_id, tenant_id); refuse with `ATHLETE_QUOTA_EXCEEDED` when cap reached (Small: 15, Medium: 250, Enterprise: unlimited)
creating → refuse when medical_* fields are set AND `athlete_medical_tracking` entitlement is off for the tenant (returns `ATHLETE_MEDICAL_TRACKING_NOT_ENABLED`)
creating → refuse when medical_* fields are set AND caller does NOT carry `athlete.manage.medical` (returns `ATHLETE_MEDICAL_PERMISSION_REQUIRED`)
creating → refuse when emergency_contact_* fields are set AND caller does NOT carry `athlete.manage.emergency_contact` (returns `ATHLETE_EMERGENCY_CONTACT_PERMISSION_REQUIRED`)
creating → set status='active' when unset; refuse status='archived' or 'graduated' or 'withdrawn' on create (terminal states cannot be initial)

creating → resolve current_age_group_id via AgeGroupResolver::resolveFor(dob, sport_key=null, on_date=today) when unset; when the resolver returns null (DOB outside every catalog), the column stays null but the observer fires a soft-warning metric
creating → set current_age_group_snapshot_at = now() when current_age_group_id is set
creating → validate consent flags: when any consent_* is true, consent_recorded_at MUST be set AND consent_recorded_by_user_id MUST be set

creating → for minors (age_years < config.athlete.minor_min_age), when any consent_* is true, refuse when consent_recorded_by_user_id is not a User with an active AthleteGuardian row on this athlete + has_legal_custody=true (Wave 3a: soft-warn; Wave 3b: hard-fail via `consent_recorded_by_authorised_guardian` rule once AthleteGuardian module exists)
creating → fire AthleteCreated
creating → fire AthleteLinkedToUser when user_id is non-null (initial link)
updating date_of_birth → REFUSE (returns `ATHLETE_DOB_IMMUTABLE`). Age-eligibility criticality — typo fix requires archive + recreate (or super_admin override event, Wave 3+)

updating user_id null → non-null → allow (initial link path — the /link-user endpoint); refuse when target user.tenant_id != tenant_id; refuse when another live Athlete row exists for (tenant_id, user_id); fire AthleteLinkedToUser
updating user_id non-null → non-null (different value) → REFUSE (returns `ATHLETE_USER_ID_IMMUTABLE`). Post-first-link the user pointer is frozen
updating user_id non-null → null → REFUSE (returns `ATHLETE_USER_UNLINK_FORBIDDEN`). Unlinking a user is a compliance smell (audit-trail launder); the correct path is archive + recreate
updating branch_id → REFUSE in Wave 3a (returns `ATHLETE_BRANCH_IMMUTABLE_WAVE_3A`). Wave 3b ships the transfer flow with `athlete_branches` multi-branch pivot

updating first_name / last_name / preferred_name / gender / pronouns / height_cm / weight_kg / dominant_hand / primary_language / profile_photo_url / registration_notes / metadata / external_id → fire AthleteUpdated with changed_fields

updating any medical_* field → refuse when `athlete_medical_tracking` entitlement is off; refuse when caller does NOT carry `athlete.manage.medical`; fire AthleteMedicalUpdated (private-tier event, routes to medical-audit queue); write to the dedicated `athlete_medical_history` audit table (SEPARATE from the normal `audits` table — 7-year regulated-health retention)

updating any emergency_contact_* field → refuse when caller does NOT carry `athlete.manage.emergency_contact`; fire AthleteEmergencyContactUpdated (audit + activity feed WITHOUT the values — activity shows 'emergency contact updated', not the phone number)
updating any consent_* field: validate consent_recorded_at + consent_recorded_by_user_id are set when flag flips false → true
updating consent_photo_release specifically → fire AthletePhotoConsentChanged (drives marketing cascade)
updating any consent_* false → true → fire AthleteConsentRecorded
updating any consent_* true → false → fire AthleteConsentRevoked per flag revoked

updating status active → paused → refuse when paused_reason is unset (returns `ATHLETE_PAUSE_REASON_REQUIRED`); set paused_at = now(); fire AthletePausedForSafeguarding; cascade — Wave 4+: pause future SessionBookings referencing this athlete
updating status paused → active → clear paused_at + paused_reason; fire AthleteReturnedFromPause

updating status active → graduated → refuse when active enrollments exist AND caller did not confirm cascade (returns `ATHLETE_HAS_ACTIVE_ENROLLMENTS`); set graduated_at = today() when unset; release 1 unit of athlete_slot; fire AthleteGraduated

updating status active/paused → withdrawn → refuse when withdrawal_reason is unset (returns `ATHLETE_WITHDRAWAL_REASON_REQUIRED`); refuse when active enrollments exist AND caller did not confirm cascade (returns `ATHLETE_HAS_ACTIVE_ENROLLMENTS`); set withdrawn_at = today(); release 1 unit of athlete_slot; fire AthleteWithdrawn
updating status {active, paused, graduated, withdrawn} → archived → refuse when active AthleteEnrollment rows reference (Wave 3a sibling); release 1 unit of athlete_slot; fire AthleteArchived

updating status archived → active (restore) → refuse when the user_id conflicts with another live athlete in the tenant; refuse when the age is now outside platform bounds (>100); consume 1 unit of athlete_slot; fire AthleteRestored; recompute age-group snapshot

updating current_age_group_id → allow ONLY from the RollAthleteAgeGroupSnapshotJob context (checked via `Bus::isDispatchedFromJob(RollAthleteAgeGroupSnapshotJob::class)` or SetAgeGroupSnapshotOnAthleteCreated hook). Manual writes refused with `ATHLETE_AGE_GROUP_SNAPSHOT_WRITE_FORBIDDEN`
updating current_age_group_id (from the job) → fire AthleteAgeGroupSnapshotUpdated
deleting (soft-delete) → refuse when status NOT IN ('archived') — must transition to archived first (returns `ATHLETE_MUST_BE_ARCHIVED_BEFORE_DELETE`)
deleting (soft-delete) → refuse when active AthleteEnrollment rows reference (returns `ATHLETE_HAS_ACTIVE_ENROLLMENTS`)

deleting (hard-delete) → RESTRICT while any AthleteGuardian row references (they cascade delete on Athlete hard-delete — the hook fires); RESTRICT while any AthleteEnrollment.athlete_id references (Wave 3a sibling); RESTRICT while any finance::Membership.athlete_id references (Wave 4+); allow only after 7-year retention

post-commit invariant recheck → if user_id is set + another live row now exists for (tenant_id, user_id) (race), fire diagnostic event; this should never happen because the observer uses SELECT ... FOR UPDATE inside the transaction
